Immediately after the first successtul test of the technology; the Star Leaqua began t0 build HPG stations on planets across know space: ComStar still operates most of the functioning HPGs, almost all ot which trace their origins to Star League tmes. ComStar inherited the HPG network following the fall of the Star League; It held monopoly on interstellar communicatlons for almost two Fand = half centuries, until the events of recent years sundered the organization. The Word ot Blake, ComStars rival splinter group , currently controls  sizable portion of the HPG network The HPG network consists ol transmitreceive stations on most inhabited worlds. Stations receive "A or "B status; based malnly upon the size Of world's population: Class stations are the network $ main hubs, while Class stations form the nodes. Each station transmits all the messages has been paid send to all other stations within its range. Those stations then add their own messages to the batch and pass them on to the next stations Iine, and so on, until the messages reach their final destination. Though sending hundreds and thousands of copies of the same message around the galaxy may appear inefficient, ensures that messages reach their destinations as swiftly as poS- sible. To avoid confusion, each station automatically deletes messages that have ready passed through its system: Each station normally transmits on set schedule_ Messages are processed serially and placed in queue until they can be transmitted. Class stations transmit the most often, usually once every six hours. Class stations transmit far less frequently: During Star League times_ they transmitted once every twelve hours; today most stations average transmissions only once every few days. The actual schedule depends Mainly on the number of waiting messages and how many other stations are within range Sometimes, transmissions can be agonizingly slow: message from Tharkad takes an average of six days to reach Terra; the same mes- sage might take months reach the Mos distant Periphery world. Priority transmissions can be sent more quickly, but at a significantly high- er cost because the stations that pass the message must be aligned correctly:

Each prefecture mints its own coins and notes but there is lit- tle difference among them beyond the name of the mint, which embossed on the edge of coins or as watermark on notes,

Those who travel regularly have several other options_ Frequent travelers can increase the range of worlds where their card accepted usually for fee of about ,000 Cbills per world per year. They arrange shadov accounts' with local banks the records of which are reconciled with their home bank ComStar'$ HPG network. such accounts are usually checked only once week , is possible defraud the system cloned" cards; however;   sophisticated electronic sale guards the card keep such criminal activity to minimum; Accounts with ComStar; reconciled daily, are securer and are also accepted throughout the Inner Sphere. C bills are accepted virtually everywhere , without the conversion penalties that may accrue using H-bill accounts in aother realm The high cost of these accounts_ however blanket fee of 10,000 Cbills per year usually restricts  them trading companies; mercenary units and other large groups rather than private individuals frequent traveler may also carry certified card, which does not have corresponding account: Instead_ funds are
ComStar C bills make up roughly ten percent of the currency in the Inner Sphere Originally intended as medium of payment for ComStar'$ services they have become widely accepted across the Inner Sphere and Periphery; serving as second currency in most states: As the stablest of the Inner Sphere currencies the C bill is ideal for internationa transactions Some have sug; gested adopting the C bill as the currency of the new Star League_ but this dea has met with considerable resistance. bills are mainly used in electronic format; but hard money exists in 1, 5, 10, 50 and 100 C-bill notes_ Several coins known as demis, are used in ComStar-administered territories but their use outside these areas limited_ Instead_ smaller sums are usually handled in the local currency:
